name: Achenbach, C. H.
description: Sometimes he's mad, Sometimes he's sad, Sometimes he's glad, Sometimes he's bad.
text: Sometimes he's mad, Sometimes he's sad, Sometimes he's glad, Sometimes he's bad.

name: Anderson, Willie
motto: "Would that I were a boy again."
description: Bill has had a hard time all year convincing people that she is a Senior, and she has had to talk so much in defending herself that some are still of the opinion that she is a Freshman.
text:
"Would that I were a boy again."
Bill has had a hard time all year convincing people that she is a Senior, and she has had to talk so much in defending herself that some are still of the opinion that she is a Freshman.

name: Anderson, L. I.
description: He'd be less of a puzzle If on his mouth he wore a muzzle.
text: He'd be less of a puzzle If on his mouth he wore a muzzle.

name: Atkinson, Elza
motto: "Every effort we make for the happiness of others lifts us above ourselves."
description: Elza is a loyal wide-awake girl who is liked by everybody, and who does all she can, in the very best way she can.
text:
"Every effort we make for the happiness of others lifts us above ourselves."
Elza is a loyal wide-awake girl who is liked by everybody, and who does all she can, in the very best way she can.

name: Ball, S. M.
motto: To meet, to know, to love, and then to part, Is the happy tale of Ball's and Barton's hearts.
text: To meet, to know, to love, and then to part, Is the happy tale of Ball's and Barton's hearts.

name: Barton, W. H.
motto: One pound of learning requires ten pounds of common sense to apply it. A pretty girl with a pretty voice Is likely to be the girl of his choice.
text: One pound of learning requires ten pounds of common sense to apply it. A pretty girl with a pretty voice Is likely to be the girl of his choice.

name: Bayley, P. L.
motto: A mathematician and musician fine, And breaks the Golden Rule "nary" a time.
text: A mathematician and musician fine, And breaks the Golden Rule "nary" a time.

name: Belts, Florence
motto: Polite and precise and pious, Small and smart and silent. "Wouldn't you be shocked to see her rooting at a football game?"
text: Polite and precise and pious, Small and smart and silent. "Wouldn't you be shocked to see her rooting at a football game?"

name: Blackshire, L. Ochie
motto: “Our deeds still travel with us from afar, And what we have been makes us what we are.”
text: “Our deeds still travel with us from afar, And what we have been makes us what we are.”

name: Blakely, L. R. K. A.
motto: “The dearest remembrance will still be the last, His sweetest memorial, the first kiss of love.” “Since he on honey dew hath fed, And drunk the milk of paradise.”
text: “The dearest remembrance will still be the last, His sweetest memorial, the first kiss of love.” “Since he on honey dew hath fed, And drunk the milk of paradise.”

name: Bledsoe, Alva
description: When she came among us she had a wonderful brain, but her troubles and tussles with “math” have been recompensed by giving her a fine, clear and extensive prospect.
text: When she came among us she had a wonderful brain, but her troubles and tussles with “math” have been recompensed by giving her a fine, clear and extensive prospect.

name: Brereton, Blanche
motto: “The winds and waves are always on the side of the ablest navigator.”
description: Blanche is a girl who never fails to find a solution for any problem she meets.
text:
“The winds and waves are always on the side of the ablest navigator.”
Blanche is a girl who never fails to find a solution for any problem she meets.

name: Brewer, M. H.
description: As an instructor Mack is an indispensable acquisition to the Military Department. He executes all movements with the ease and grace of a cow.
text: As an instructor Mack is an indispensable acquisition to the Military Department. He executes all movements with the ease and grace of a cow.

name: Buckley, S. S.
motto: "Equal rights to all and special privileges to none."
description: Spence is a jolly good fellow, perfectly capable of running Spence's affairs.
text:
"Equal rights to all and special privileges to none."
Spence is a jolly good fellow, perfectly capable of running Spence's affairs.

name: Bullock, T. J.
description: Given the appellation, "Mamma's boy," by the Model School children. His crushing personality would break a stone structure. Oldest man in the University. Favors the girls in all his teaching.
text: Given the appellation, "Mamma's boy," by the Model School children. His crushing personality would break a stone structure. Oldest man in the University. Favors the girls in all his teaching.

name: Cherry, J. L.
motto: "The girls, they chuckle me under the chin, To see the black-headed Agri grin."
description: Louis is a farmer by profession, a lady's man by practice and a preacher's son by merit. Louis also holds some claims as a poet, being the author of:
text:
"The girls, they chuckle me under the chin, To see the black-headed Agri grin."
Louis is a farmer by profession, a lady's man by practice and a preacher's son by merit. Louis also holds some claims as a poet, being the author of:

name: Deane, Madeline
motto: "Common sense in an uncommon degree is what the world calls wisdom."
description: Madeline has this in abundance, besides a big heart, ready sympathy and unselfish nature that have won her many friends.
text:
"Common sense in an uncommon degree is what the world calls wisdom."
Madeline has this in abundance, besides a big heart, ready sympathy and unselfish nature that have won her many friends.

name: Deeg, Lena
motto: "Things don't turn up in this world until somebody turns them up."
description: Lena is destined to be a shining light in politics, and we feel that in her future career she will turn things up just as successfully as of old at the U. of A.
text:
"Things don't turn up in this world until somebody turns them up."
Lena is destined to be a shining light in politics, and we feel that in her future career she will turn things up just as successfully as of old at the U. of A.

name: Dickinson, R. V.
description: Dick, as he is commonly called, is noted for his church-going propensity. He has been one time since he has been in the University, and that was when the Christian Church burned.
text: Dick, as he is commonly called, is noted for his church-going propensity. He has been one time since he has been in the University, and that was when the Christian Church burned.

name: Douglas, W. E.
description: Tovey's pet, whom he brought down from Galesburg for company, and who once saved the famous musician's life on the Lakes. Plays the clarinet for fun and livelihood.
text: Tovey's pet, whom he brought down from Galesburg for company, and who once saved the famous musician's life on the Lakes. Plays the clarinet for fun and livelihood.

name: Droke, Mary
motto: "Force rules the world, not opinion; but opinion which makes use of force."
description: Mary's strong points are her good looks and brains. She is a spark in Math. and a prodigy in French.
text:
"Force rules the world, not opinion; but opinion which makes use of force."
Mary's strong points are her good looks and brains. She is a spark in Math. and a prodigy in French.

name: Gist, J. E.
description: Gist has a great ambition. Since he was sixteen his highest aspiration has been to become President—of a family.
text: Gist has a great ambition. Since he was sixteen his highest aspiration has been to become President—of a family.

name: Gladson, Hazel
motto: "A woman of meditation is happy, not for an hour or a day, but quite around the circle of all her years."
text: "A woman of meditation is happy, not for an hour or a day, but quite around the circle of all her years."

name: Greig, Star
description: Star maintains that 'tis the noblest profession of them all to till the soil. We predict much success for him in that line of work, for he sleeps too late for breakfast two mornings out of three.
text: Star maintains that 'tis the noblest profession of them all to till the soil. We predict much success for him in that line of work, for he sleeps too late for breakfast two mornings out of three.

name: Hamilton, A. C.
description: Three-thirds, and blame near four-thirds, of the Military Department when he was young.
text: Three-thirds, and blame near four-thirds, of the Military Department when he was young.

name: Harris, Martha
description: There is a grace and gentleness about her that always shows itself wherever she may be. She is musically inclined, and a good student of English.
text: There is a grace and gentleness about her that always shows itself wherever she may be. She is musically inclined, and a good student of English.

name: Highfill, Leroy
clubs: Business manager of the Cardinal for 1911-12
description: LeRoy is a news rustler. He dreams of the day when he will become rich by writing agricultural articles. He deals wholly with the theoretical and leaves the other man to work with the practical.
text: LeRoy is a news rustler. He dreams of the day when he will become rich by writing agricultural articles. He deals wholly with the theoretical and leaves the other man to work with the practical.

name: Holtzclaw, H. F.
description: Henry is not known to all, being Mamma's baby boy. He says, however, when he is a grown-up man he intends to draw all men unto him by teaching Math.
text: Henry is not known to all, being Mamma's baby boy. He says, however, when he is a grown-up man he intends to draw all men unto him by teaching Math.

name: Horton, H. W.
description: Here by mistake. This is his first year in Arkansas. Quiet, jovial, good-natured, and worthy of serious consideration.
text: Here by mistake. This is his first year in Arkansas. Quiet, jovial, good-natured, and worthy of serious consideration.

name: Horton, Ralph
motto: "an honest confession is good for the soul."
description: The man who never sleeps. Not because he doesn't want to, but because of his dreams over Latin 2. He fully believes that "an honest confession is good for the soul." Because of such belief, he told Prof. Futrall he didn't care anything for Latin, except he wanted a pass. He Passed. P stands for pass, you know.
text:
"an honest confession is good for the soul."
The man who never sleeps. Not because he doesn't want to, but because of his dreams over Latin 2. He fully believes that "an honest confession is good for the soul." Because of such belief, he told Prof. Futrall he didn't care anything for Latin, except he wanted a pass. He Passed. P stands for pass, you know.

name: Hurst, J. D.
description: There are only two courses that Davis can boast of, and they are German and History 7. He coaches German and knocks on History 7.
text: There are only two courses that Davis can boast of, and they are German and History 7. He coaches German and knocks on History 7.

name: Kelton, Fannie
motto: "little, nameless, unremembered acts of kindness and of love."
description: A kind heart is a fountain of gladness, making everything in its vicinity to freshen and smile. The best portion of a good woman's life is her "little, nameless, unremembered acts of kindness and of love."
text:
"little, nameless, unremembered acts of kindness and of love."
A kind heart is a fountain of gladness, making everything in its vicinity to freshen and smile. The best portion of a good woman's life is her "little, nameless, unremembered acts of kindness and of love."

name: Kinsworthy, B. S.
description: He made his debut as a speaker during the stirring times of the strike. He intends to be a lawyer, and figure in everything from how to rob the dead to how to enrich the "malefactors of great wealth."
text: He made his debut as a speaker during the stirring times of the strike. He intends to be a lawyer, and figure in everything from how to rob the dead to how to enrich the "malefactors of great wealth."

name: Kirby, A. C.
description: Entered years ago. Will never amount to anything much, but imagines he has a voice. Insignificant, and goes among the ladies quite frequently. Expects to be a doctor.
text: Entered years ago. Will never amount to anything much, but imagines he has a voice. Insignificant, and goes among the ladies quite frequently. Expects to be a doctor.

name: Langston, Zora
description: Always patient and always kind. She meets her tasks without a murmur, and is never too busy to give a pleasant word or smile to anyone who may pass her way.
text: Always patient and always kind. She meets her tasks without a murmur, and is never too busy to give a pleasant word or smile to anyone who may pass her way.

name: Lawson, Lillian
motto: "The sunshine is but rivalled by the sunshine of her hair."
description: Lillian has style of dress and style of manner, but neither of these surpass the style of her writing. Among her inexpressible charms are her good looks, pleasant smile, and kind disposition.
text:
"The sunshine is but rivalled by the sunshine of her hair."
Lillian has style of dress and style of manner, but neither of these surpass the style of her writing. Among her inexpressible charms are her good looks, pleasant smile, and kind disposition.

name: Lucas, Camille
description: Favorite type, brunette; favorite color, blue; favorite song, "Watch On the Rhine." Camille is full of smiles and wiles, and possesses an equal amount of earnestness and sincerity.
text: Favorite type, brunette; favorite color, blue; favorite song, "Watch On the Rhine." Camille is full of smiles and wiles, and possesses an equal amount of earnestness and sincerity.

name: Marsh, J. E.
description: Jim is a "straight-from-the-shoulder" boy. He is intense in his likes and in his dislikes. At one time he studied Math., but finding it too easy for Prof. Droke, he dropped it and took Economics to Dr. Brough. Jim is frequently spoken of as "Deacon Marsh," because of his persistency in giving moral lectures to the boys.
text: Jim is a "straight-from-the-shoulder" boy. He is intense in his likes and in his dislikes. At one time he studied Math., but finding it too easy for Prof. Droke, he dropped it and took Economics to Dr. Brough. Jim is frequently spoken of as "Deacon Marsh," because of his persistency in giving moral lectures to the boys.

name: McCluer, R. D.
description: Can learn absolutely nothing but rocks. Believes in evolution; his father is a minister, but Mac. is evolving backwards. What he doesn't know about fossils was torn out of the book.
text: Can learn absolutely nothing but rocks. Believes in evolution; his father is a minister, but Mac. is evolving backwards. What he doesn't know about fossils was torn out of the book.

name: McLelland, C. J.
description: An incomparable phenomenon commonly known as "Ram." Mac hates his enemies, but sticks "closer than a brother" to his friends. He has an abundance of self-confidence, and is always ready to assist in any good cause. Rather than be in the limelight himself, he prefers to be behind the man in the limelight.
text: An incomparable phenomenon commonly known as "Ram." Mac hates his enemies, but sticks "closer than a brother" to his friends. He has an abundance of self-confidence, and is always ready to assist in any good cause. Rather than be in the limelight himself, he prefers to be behind the man in the limelight.

name: Moore, J. G. Lee
clubs: President of Y. M. C. A.
description: The wittiest, prettiest, most popular boy in school. Very fond of Latin and Agriculture. Doesn't know what he will be, but expects to be self-sustaining, which his friends very much doubt. A man thoroughly sound in all his principles.
text: The wittiest, prettiest, most popular boy in school. Very fond of Latin and Agriculture. Doesn't know what he will be, but expects to be self-sustaining, which his friends very much doubt. A man thoroughly sound in all his principles.

name: Moore, S. W.
motto: "He whistles when he's merry, he whistles when he's sad, he whistles when the weather's fine, he whistles when it's bad."
description: Sam has more pressing engagements than any U. of A. boy. But amid it all.
text:
"He whistles when he's merry, he whistles when he's sad, he whistles when the weather's fine, he whistles when it's bad."
Sam has more pressing engagements than any U. of A. boy. But amid it all.

name: Morton, Jennie
description: Unlike Tennyson's Brook, she flows on as a calm, steady river, without a single bubbling current. We may safely say that her dignity is her major.
text: Unlike Tennyson's Brook, she flows on as a calm, steady river, without a single bubbling current. We may safely say that her dignity is her major.

name: Moss, Lowell
motto: "In him the virtues congregate."
description: A good little boy, his teachers say. Never makes any noise, and will play all day all to himself.
text:
"In him the virtues congregate."
A good little boy, his teachers say. Never makes any noise, and will play all day all to himself.

name: Newberry, J. L.
motto: "Things are not always what they seem."
description: He is a cartoonist of first rank. Best fellow in the world to make excuses, and the poorest fellow in the world to take abuses. Fish climbs the ladder of honor and fame in his dreams. 'Tis good he does, if he hopes to climb.
text:
"Things are not always what they seem."
He is a cartoonist of first rank. Best fellow in the world to make excuses, and the poorest fellow in the world to take abuses. Fish climbs the ladder of honor and fame in his dreams. 'Tis good he does, if he hopes to climb.

name: Northum, T. M.
description: Quiet and unassuming, but puts through everything he undertakes. His neatness makes him a lion among the ladies. Guilty of efficient service on the Dormitory Council.
text: Quiet and unassuming, but puts through everything he undertakes. His neatness makes him a lion among the ladies. Guilty of efficient service on the Dormitory Council.

name: Oneal, F. L.
description: Although not one of the "Dirty Thirty-Six," he believes in "turning on the light." He has more practical experience than any other E. E. Never been with a girl in his life, but expects to marry a suffragette.
text: Although not one of the "Dirty Thirty-Six," he believes in "turning on the light." He has more practical experience than any other E. E. Never been with a girl in his life, but expects to marry a suffragette.

name: Orton, Myrtle
description: Intelligence is a luxury. It is a torch or a firebrand, according to the use one makes of it. With Myrtle it is a "Torch."
text: Intelligence is a luxury. It is a torch or a firebrand, according to the use one makes of it. With Myrtle it is a "Torch."

name: Overton, W. R. Lee
description: His quietness is exceeded only by the whiteness of his hair. Went to classes six years without missing one until he broke his leg. Believes in woman suffrage. Surveyed Prof. Droke's turnip patch for a pass in Mathematics.
text: His quietness is exceeded only by the whiteness of his hair. Went to classes six years without missing one until he broke his leg. Believes in woman suffrage. Surveyed Prof. Droke's turnip patch for a pass in Mathematics.

name: Parcell, E. W.
description: Eats Math as vivaciously as he does bananas. Although from a warm country, he expects to stay away hereafter. Good fellow, but few know it.
text: Eats Math as vivaciously as he does bananas. Although from a warm country, he expects to stay away hereafter. Good fellow, but few know it.

name: Potter, Mabel
motto: "Still water runs deep."
description: Mabel is a splendid little housekeeper, and it has been rumored that she is a candidate for a degree in Domestic Science.
text:
"Still water runs deep."
Mabel is a splendid little housekeeper, and it has been rumored that she is a candidate for a degree in Domestic Science.

name: Potter, Winnie
description: Winnie can't decide whether she wants to be a Prof. of Latin in a higher university or a hair dresser in some New York establishment, so she is going to stay another year to think the matter over.
text: Winnie can't decide whether she wants to be a Prof. of Latin in a higher university or a hair dresser in some New York establishment, so she is going to stay another year to think the matter over.

name: Potter, R. L.
description: To find out more about him, ask his guardian, Mrs. R. L. Potter.
text: To find out more about him, ask his guardian, Mrs. R. L. Potter.

name: Rhyne, J. O.
clubs: Representative of the Senior Class to the Student Council
description: Jake has won an enviable reputation for his good, easy nature. He offends no one, and is not easily offended. Yet he is a football player, paradoxical as it may seem.
text: Jake has won an enviable reputation for his good, easy nature. He offends no one, and is not easily offended. Yet he is a football player, paradoxical as it may seem.

name: Rice, Edna
description: She is industrious, studious, and intellectual. After finishing here she intends to teach for a while, and later found an orphans' asylum.
text: She is industrious, studious, and intellectual. After finishing here she intends to teach for a while, and later found an orphans' asylum.

name: Richmond, H.
description: Commonly known as "Prep," a title entirely fitting. Is learning mechanics to enable him to build fires in his dad's hardware store. He never comes to the Main Building except for Math., nor has he seen a girl since he had English 1. Works hard, and is a great standby with his Professors.
text: Commonly known as "Prep," a title entirely fitting. Is learning mechanics to enable him to build fires in his dad's hardware store. He never comes to the Main Building except for Math., nor has he seen a girl since he had English 1. Works hard, and is a great standby with his Professors.

name: Roark, G. W.
description: Price is his main consideration, although he is not a merchant. Has a bunch of formulas stored away in his little bump of knowledge. Being Dr. Brough's brother-in-law is an asset to him. Has trouble saying things for his "ahs!"
text: Price is his main consideration, although he is not a merchant. Has a bunch of formulas stored away in his little bump of knowledge. Being Dr. Brough's brother-in-law is an asset to him. Has trouble saying things for his "ahs!"

name: Schalchlin, G. W.
description: One of Arkansas' greatest quarterbacks and shortstops. Very affable. His home is Mablevale, but his pursuit is Mable Constant.
text: One of Arkansas' greatest quarterbacks and shortstops. Very affable. His home is Mablevale, but his pursuit is Mable Constant.

name: Shannon, Mary
motto: "When you get me a good man made of arguments I will get you a good dinner made by reading a cookery book."
description: She is a girl who never fails to express her own opinion, for—what her heart thinketh her tongue speaketh.
text:
"When you get me a good man made of arguments I will get you a good dinner made by reading a cookery book."
She is a girl who never fails to express her own opinion, for—what her heart thinketh her tongue speaketh.

name: Skinner, B. J.
description: Dad takes great interest in his real estate on Pea Ridge. He can never laugh without showing his artificiality.
text: Dad takes great interest in his real estate on Pea Ridge. He can never laugh without showing his artificiality.

name: Snell, Edith
description: Edith trembles at her own capability, but we notice that she does all things well. Some think her timid, but on knowing her they find that she is firm.
text: Edith trembles at her own capability, but we notice that she does all things well. Some think her timid, but on knowing her they find that she is firm.

name: Stockburger, R. R.
description: His parents say he was always handsome, even as a baby.
text: His parents say he was always handsome, even as a baby.

name: Stover, D. A.
motto: "The truth itself is not believed."
description: Some question Don's being a Senior. Became a valuable asset to the team. Against his theology to have scruples. If he has not "done" you he will, if you get acquainted with him.
text:
"The truth itself is not believed."
Some question Don's being a Senior. Became a valuable asset to the team. Against his theology to have scruples. If he has not "done" you he will, if you get acquainted with him.

name: Strickland, George
description: He says he's not well up in English, but he's an Agri. born, and an Agri. bred to raise the corn to make the bread for the new generation, the foundation of this wonderful nation.
text: He says he's not well up in English, but he's an Agri. born, and an Agri. bred to raise the corn to make the bread for the new generation, the foundation of this wonderful nation.

name: Tilley, Irene, A. Y.
motto: "And she has smiles to earth unknown, Smiles that with motions of their own, Do spread and sink and rise, That come and go with endless play, And ever as they pass away Are hidden in her eyes."
text: "And she has smiles to earth unknown, Smiles that with motions of their own, Do spread and sink and rise, That come and go with endless play, And ever as they pass away Are hidden in her eyes."

name: Torrence, J. H.
description: Torrence is an indefatigable worker. He reads Chemistry for pastime.
text: Torrence is an indefatigable worker. He reads Chemistry for pastime.

name: Tucker, M. C.
clubs: Represents the farmers on the Student Council
description: Married. and can put more into a big horn than he can get out in a week.
text: Married. and can put more into a big horn than he can get out in a week.

name: Waldron, R. C.
description: which he says is the first step toward the U. S. Senate.
text: which he says is the first step toward the U. S. Senate.

name: Williams, Maurice
description: Maurice was accused in his Junior year of being a ladies' man. He has not yet rid himself of the charge.
text: Maurice was accused in his Junior year of being a ladies' man. He has not yet rid himself of the charge.

name: Wood, R. W.
description: Roy could be the best all-round athlete in Arkansas, but, strange to say, he takes very little interest in athletics.
text: Roy could be the best all-round athlete in Arkansas, but, strange to say, he takes very little interest in athletics.

name: Woody, W. W.
description: Most commonly known as Judge Woody. His catchy looks would chill the hottest idea of his satanic majesty. "Woody" never talks, but thinks on facts.
text: Most commonly known as Judge Woody. His catchy looks would chill the hottest idea of his satanic majesty. "Woody" never talks, but thinks on facts.

name: Wylie, C. N.
clubs: President of the class during his Junior year
description: Colen prides himself as being Lieutenant Wylie. His black eyes and hair are killers, so the ladies say. While Colen is a B. A., he feels that he has an interest in the Agriculture Department.
text: Colen prides himself as being Lieutenant Wylie. His black eyes and hair are killers, so the ladies say. While Colen is a B. A., he feels that he has an interest in the Agriculture Department.

name: Acree, W. F.
description: A friend to every Freshman. They should not be hazed.
text: A friend to every Freshman. They should not be hazed.

name: Barton, Alma
motto: Modesty is the brightest jewel in the crown of womankind.
text: Modesty is the brightest jewel in the crown of womankind.

name: Adams, Elizabeth
motto: The maiden whose head is splintered with Cupid's dart.
text: The maiden whose head is splintered with Cupid's dart.

name: Berry, B. M.
motto: Is seen but little, and known less.
text: Is seen but little, and known less.

name: Andrews, M.
description: The quietest man in Gray Hall.
text: The quietest man in Gray Hall.

name: Bird, Nelle
motto: "No, my name is not Nellie, neither is it spelled B-y-r-d. I'm a real bird. Have you that fifty cents for class dues?"
text: "No, my name is not Nellie, neither is it spelled B-y-r-d. I'm a real bird. Have you that fifty cents for class dues?"

name: Armitage, Rita
motto: "I can love more than those girls who love so easily and so often."
text: "I can love more than those girls who love so easily and so often."

name: Bowen, E. A.
motto: "Behold how straight I walk."
text: "Behold how straight I walk."

name: Banta, Katherine
description: The charming blonde who has been so interested in the Cardinal and the Editor-in-Chief. She says they go well together.
text: The charming blonde who has been so interested in the Cardinal and the Editor-in-Chief. She says they go well together.

name: Boyd, Francis
description: She may some day go to the Hall of Fame as a poetess, if she continues making rhymes.
text: She may some day go to the Hall of Fame as a poetess, if she continues making rhymes.

name: Bradley, H. H.
motto: "Let's see; importance sawed off."
text: "Let's see; importance sawed off."

name: Carroll, Hugh A. Dinsmore
motto: "It is not all in a man, but part in a name."
text: "It is not all in a man, but part in a name."

name: Bragg, P. N.
motto: "Let time that makes you homely, make you sage."
text: "Let time that makes you homely, make you sage."

name: Casey, W. B.
motto: "All his beauty and glory come from within."
text: "All his beauty and glory come from within."

name: Bryant, Anna
motto: "If winsome smiles and artificial dimples won't smash his heart, what will it take?"
text: "If winsome smiles and artificial dimples won't smash his heart, what will it take?"

name: Clark, M. D.
motto: "Has not been seen lately; must be sleeping."
text: "Has not been seen lately; must be sleeping."

name: Buerkle, J. G.
motto: "I am going to buy a conservative suit this spring. I am tired of these fussy ones."
text: "I am going to buy a conservative suit this spring. I am tired of these fussy ones."

name: Cook, E. T.
motto: "What, me take a woman? Never!"
text: "What, me take a woman? Never!"

name: Carnes, G. C.
motto: "They'll trade latht with you."
description: He dances as though he were an ethereal being.
text:
"They'll trade latht with you."
He dances as though he were an ethereal being.

name: Coventon, J. W.
description: Jim Marsh's room-mate.
text: Jim Marsh's room-mate.

name: Croom, S. G.
motto: "I am a man; what concerns man must concern me."
text: "I am a man; what concerns man must concern me."

name: Downs, R. R.
description: Yes, this is he, Roy Railroad Rodman Robert Downs.
text: Yes, this is he, Roy Railroad Rodman Robert Downs.

name: Crumpler, S. A.
motto: "Gone but not forgotten."
text: "Gone but not forgotten."

name: Duncan, W. W.
motto: "Mexico for mine as soon as I can leave this place."
text: "Mexico for mine as soon as I can leave this place."

name: Davenport, Bess
description: She is a Latin shark, and consequently Professor Futrall's pet.
text: She is a Latin shark, and consequently Professor Futrall's pet.

name: Dunn, W. A.
description: He has done nothing to tell about.
text: He has done nothing to tell about.

name: Devaney, Hallie
description: Light of heart—and head.
text: Light of heart—and head.

name: Earl, R. D.
motto: "Time is lord of thee; Thy wealth, thy glory, And thy name are his."
text: "Time is lord of thee; Thy wealth, thy glory, And thy name are his."

name: Dowdle, R. G.
motto: "Great thoughts, like great deeds, need no trumpet."
text: "Great thoughts, like great deeds, need no trumpet."

name: Ellis, Elizabeth
description: If hot air were music she would be a brass band.
text: If hot air were music she would be a brass band.

name: Funk, Gladys
motto: "Where's that racket?"
description: "Turn off the light, girls, I hear G. Funk coming." "She comes, she sees, she proctors." She will have at least one star in her crown for her unswerving devotion to duty as Chief Proctor in Carnall Hall for two years in succession.
text:
"Where's that racket?"
"Turn off the light, girls, I hear G. Funk coming." "She comes, she sees, she proctors." She will have at least one star in her crown for her unswerving devotion to duty as Chief Proctor in Carnall Hall for two years in succession.

name: Hays, C. W.
motto: "I didn't understand the question."
text: "I didn't understand the question."

name: Gerig, F. A.
motto: "Doubt that the stars are fire. Doubt that the sun doth move. Doubt truth to be a liar, But never doubt I love."
text: "Doubt that the stars are fire. Doubt that the sun doth move. Doubt truth to be a liar, But never doubt I love."

name: Heagler, E. A.
description: He arrived just in time to get his name in.
text: He arrived just in time to get his name in.

name: Gordon, J. H.
motto: "Oh, durn it."
description: Expects to fall heir to Mack Brewer's blue suit.
text:
"Oh, durn it."
Expects to fall heir to Mack Brewer's blue suit.

name: Henry, E. A.
description: The man who said: "He who steals my purse steals trash."
text: The man who said: "He who steals my purse steals trash."

name: Goss, A. L.
motto: "I had rather be a moving picture machine operator than President."
text: "I had rather be a moving picture machine operator than President."

name: Holcomb, Lillian
motto: "Mother of God! It is like a thousand deaths when one can not see the face one wants."
text: "Mother of God! It is like a thousand deaths when one can not see the face one wants."

name: Graham, J. J.
description: A perpetual motion fiend.
text: A perpetual motion fiend.

name: Hallabaugh, Essie
motto: "If my man don't take to bein' more 'konstant' he'll git fewer letters."
text: "If my man don't take to bein' more 'konstant' he'll git fewer letters."

name: Holt, L. M.
description: Was seen in the main building one time this year.
text: Was seen in the main building one time this year.

name: Johnson, Nelle
motto: "It is not work that makes the student; it is scheming."
text: "It is not work that makes the student; it is scheming."

name: Holt, J. B.
motto: "They do not love that do not show their love."
text: "They do not love that do not show their love."

name: Jordan, Mary
description: Her head is as full of gold as a millionaire's purse—it is red.
text: Her head is as full of gold as a millionaire's purse—it is red.

name: Hon, Mabel
motto: "It is the greatest art on earth to tell a lie that sounds like the truth."
text: "It is the greatest art on earth to tell a lie that sounds like the truth."

name: Keith, Allen A.
description: A second Zaccheus.
text: A second Zaccheus.

name: Huntley, B. W.
motto: "Tall oaks from little acorns grow."
text: "Tall oaks from little acorns grow."

name: Keller, Fred
motto: "A stranger who strangled his language in his tears."
text: "A stranger who strangled his language in his tears."

name: Jackson, A. R.
motto: "We know not where he has gone."
text: "We know not where he has gone."

name: Kennedy, W. E.
description: A man from a far country.
text: A man from a far country.

name: Killian, Vida
description: Lucky is that Freshman, who at the proper time can pose as a Sophomore, Junior, or Senior.
text: Lucky is that Freshman, who at the proper time can pose as a Sophomore, Junior, or Senior.

name: Magness, P. G.
motto: "The Gladiator of Gray Hall, He will not be strapped. Strap Magness! Strap Magness!"
text: "The Gladiator of Gray Hall, He will not be strapped. Strap Magness! Strap Magness!"

name: Knerr, Bertha
motto: "The best part of beauty is that which a picture cannot show."
text: "The best part of beauty is that which a picture cannot show."

name: Martin, Alma
description: Here by mistake, but what can't be cured, will have to be endured.
text: Here by mistake, but what can't be cured, will have to be endured.

name: Lake, E. C.
description: He is recognized as the most popular boy in the Junior Class. "Dub" has been known as a woman hater, now he is known as a lady's man. The girls say he is a G. M.
text: He is recognized as the most popular boy in the Junior Class. "Dub" has been known as a woman hater, now he is known as a lady's man. The girls say he is a G. M.

name: May, Russell V.
description: Her eyes and the color she wears are blue; If I win the game will I win her too? If that is true, just see what I'll do.
text: Her eyes and the color she wears are blue; If I win the game will I win her too? If that is true, just see what I'll do.

name: Lamberton, H. C.
motto: "Pat—Shore an' there is nothing too good for the Irish."
text: "Pat—Shore an' there is nothing too good for the Irish."

name: McCulley, Icy Mae
description: She says that her highest ambition is to get a M. A. + N. (degree in the U. of A.)
text: She says that her highest ambition is to get a M. A. + N. (degree in the U. of A.)

name: Lacer, Lucile
description: Her art lies in her artlessness.
text: Her art lies in her artlessness.

name: Marguerite, McFarland
motto: "But at any rate I have loved the season Of Art's spring-birth, so dim and dewy."
text: "But at any rate I have loved the season Of Art's spring-birth, so dim and dewy."

name: McGaugh, Callie
description: Professor Williams says that she is the only girl who knows how to dance correctly.
text: Professor Williams says that she is the only girl who knows how to dance correctly.

name: Morehead, Louise
motto: "There is only one thing that counts; to love and be loved."
description: "Resolved, That I will catch some man, by hook or crook, the best I can." Her motto: "There is only one thing that counts; to love and be loved."
text:
"There is only one thing that counts; to love and be loved."
"Resolved, That I will catch some man, by hook or crook, the best I can." Her motto: "There is only one thing that counts; to love and be loved."

name: McGill, Minto
description: "Sure! Let the women vote, what do we care?"
text: "Sure! Let the women vote, what do we care?"

name: Moss, Mildred
description: The classical girl. To know her is to love her.
text: The classical girl. To know her is to love her.

name: McGill, S. S.
description: "Sure enough short."
text: "Sure enough short."

name: Payne, E. E.
description: "A man who is in love is not responsible for what he does."
text: "A man who is in love is not responsible for what he does."

name: McPherson, R. R.
description: "A whistling wife and a flock of sheep is the very best property that any man can keep."
text: "A whistling wife and a flock of sheep is the very best property that any man can keep."

name: Payne, H. B.
description: "The man who blushes is not quite a brute."
text: "The man who blushes is not quite a brute."

name: Mixon, Harvey
description: Harvey and Sy, the newlyweds.
text: Harvey and Sy, the newlyweds.

name: Poff, A. A.
description: "Oh! you great, big grizzly bear."
text: "Oh! you great, big grizzly bear."

name: Potter, G. C.
description: The more he studies the more he discovers his ignorance.
text: The more he studies the more he discovers his ignorance.

name: Rye, V. X.
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew."
text: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew."

name: Potter, H. N.
description: He can't tell vinegar from wine until he has swallowed it and has the colic.
text: He can't tell vinegar from wine until he has swallowed it and has the colic.

name: Scurlock, E. H.
description: The boys say he is good looking, the girls say he is handsome, the Model School children call him rosy, and his girl calls him blondy.
text: The boys say he is good looking, the girls say he is handsome, the Model School children call him rosy, and his girl calls him blondy.

name: Price, O. G.
description: Dr. Price stays at the Experiment Station, and we know but little of him.
text: Dr. Price stays at the Experiment Station, and we know but little of him.

name: Sharp, J. E.
description: He has musical aspirations on a slide trombone.
text: He has musical aspirations on a slide trombone.

name: Ratliff, E. M.
motto: "Policy consists in serving God in such manner as not to offend the Devil."
text: "Policy consists in serving God in such manner as not to offend the Devil."

name: Slayman, C. H.
description: Here by mistake.
text: Here by mistake.

name: Roys, M. B.
motto: "He bane a Swede, maby?"
text: "He bane a Swede, maby?"

name: Stout, S. R.
description: He spends his Sundays at home?
text: He spends his Sundays at home?

name: Thomas, O. C.
motto: "But that—mechanics."
description: Some one called him a Knight—
text:
"But that—mechanics."
Some one called him a Knight—

name: Volentine, Paul
motto: "That tall man who leads the band."
text: "That tall man who leads the band."

name: Thornton, R. E.
description: His highest ambition is to become chief pusher of a wheelbarrow.
text: His highest ambition is to become chief pusher of a wheelbarrow.

name: Wade, Hopkins
motto: "Heaven and Earth fight in vain against a dunce."
text: "Heaven and Earth fight in vain against a dunce."

name: Titus, I. R.
description: His voice is likened to that of an ungreased wagon wheel.
text: His voice is likened to that of an ungreased wagon wheel.

name: Waller, Ruth
description: Ruth is a Freshman in ways, A Sophomore in wisdom, A Junior enrolled, and A Senior in flirtation.
text: Ruth is a Freshman in ways, A Sophomore in wisdom, A Junior enrolled, and A Senior in flirtation.

name: Trent, Ruth
motto: "Thought is deeper than all speech. Feeling deeper than all thought."
text: "Thought is deeper than all speech. Feeling deeper than all thought."

name: Watters, R. F.
motto: "I am Sir Oracle, and when I speak let no dog bark."
text: "I am Sir Oracle, and when I speak let no dog bark."

name: Tyson, H. J.
description: He stops his watch at night to keep it from wearing out.
text: He stops his watch at night to keep it from wearing out.

name: Winfree, J. S.
description: He is 'mamma's little lady.' The girls are all crazy about him.
text: He is 'mamma's little lady.' The girls are all crazy about him.

name: Wolf, W. H.
motto: "It never troubles how many the sheep may be."
text: "It never troubles how many the sheep may be."

name: Weidemeyer, H. A.
motto: "They always talk, never think, who have the least to say."
text: "They always talk, never think, who have the least to say."

name: Woody, L. D.
description: Last, and also least.
text: Last, and also least.
